news.title: Tesla Adds Second Unsupervised Robotaxi in Dallas and Houston
news.summary: Crowdsourced Robotaxi Tracker data shows Tesla deployed one unsupervised vehicle in each of Dallas and Houston at launch on April 18, increasing to two vehicles per city by April 20. Service availability remained low at 0-2% with brief spikes.
news.impact_reason: Confirms small but growing unsupervised fleet presence in new Texas cities, signaling initial scaling momentum building on Austin operations.

news.title: Tesla Unsupervised Robotaxi Fleet Reaches 10 Vehicles in Austin
news.summary: Tesla's Austin robotaxi fleet hit 10 unsupervised Model Y vehicles, marking the highest confirmed count for driverless service in the city. This reflects ongoing ramp-up in the primary operational hub.
news.impact_reason: Confirmed growth to 10 unsupervised vehicles in Austin signals clear progress in fleet size expansion, a key gating factor for scaling velocity in the core Texas market.

news.title: Tesla Launches Unsupervised Robotaxi in Dallas and Houston
news.summary: Tesla announced unsupervised Robotaxi service rollout in Dallas and Houston on April 18, with initial geofence areas of about 25 square miles each and video evidence of driverless operation. This expands from ongoing unsupervised rides in Austin, tripling Texas active hubs.
news.impact_reason: Expands unsupervised operations to two major new Texas cities adjacent to Austin, directly accelerating regional fleet scaling despite small initial deployments.

news.title: TechCrunch Reports Tesla Robotaxi Expansion to Dallas and Houston
news.summary: Tesla expanded robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston, now operating in three Texas cities after Austin launch last year and unsupervised rides starting January 2026. Crowdsourced data shows one vehicle active in each new city versus 46 in Austin.
news.impact_reason: Validates the expansion to three Texas cities with unsupervised operations, supporting faster regional deployment velocity.
